About
This website uses cookies
We use cookies to personalise content and ads, to provide social media features and to analyse our traffic. We also share information about your use of our site with our social media, advertising and analytics partners who may combine it with other information that you’ve provided to them or that they’ve collected from your use of their services. You consent to our cookies if you continue to use our website.

Preference cookies enable a website to remember information that changes the way the website behaves or looks, like your preferred language or the region that you are in.

Statistic cookies help website owners to understand how visitors interact with websites by collecting and reporting information anonymously.

We do not use cookies of this type.

Marketing cookies are used to track visitors across websites. The intention is to display ads that are relevant and engaging for the individual user and thereby more valuable for publishers and third party advertisers.

Maximum Storage Duration: 1 dayType: HTTP Cookie

Maximum Storage Duration: PersistentType: HTML Local Storage

Unclassified cookies are cookies that we are in the process of classifying, together with the providers of individual cookies.

We do not use cookies of this type.

About
Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make a user's experience more efficient.

The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies we need your permission.

This site uses different types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third party services that appear on our pages.

You can at any time change or withdraw your consent from the Cookie Declaration on our website.

Learn more about who we are, how you can contact us and how we process personal data in our Privacy Policy.
Do not sell or share my personal information

We also share information about your use of our site with our social media, advertising and analytics partners who may combine it with other information that you’ve provided to them or that they’ve collected from your use of their services. You consent to our cookies if you continue to use our website.

Award
Winners
Magazine
Red Dot Award
Brands & Communication Design

The Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design annually recognises the best projects, designers and agencies in communication design. Designers and companies submit their campaigns and projects for evaluation by an international jury.

Participate now

University: FH JOANNEUM – University of Applied Sciences Design: Sophie Ortmeier

Brands & Communication Design

Login

Winners' Benefits

Participation in the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design offers designers the chance to receive international recognition, increased visibility and valuable industry contacts. These are the Winners’ Benefits that await you if you receive an award in the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design:

Evaluation by an international, independent jury of experts
Boost to your reputation and image
Access to an international community of designers, producers and potential customers
Global recognition as an attractive employer
Celebrating your success at the awards ceremony in Berlin
By starting the video, you agree that data, e.g. your IP address, will be sent to Vimeo. vimeo.com/privacy
Awards

In the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design, there are three distinctions. The Red Dot: Grand Prix is the top individual award and is given to a maximum of one project per category. Projects and brands that impress with their excellent design quality and creative achievements are honoured with the Red Dot: Best of the Best. The Red Dot is the award for high design quality.

Award Ceremony 2025: Lukas Cottrell (2nd from left) at the announcement of the Red Dot: Grand Prix for the project “The Revived”
Lukas Cottrell, CEO Peter Schmidt Group:

“The Red Dot Award has international reach and resonates far beyond the design industry. Our record result not only makes us proud. It also sends a strong signal to the market: strong, creative ideas give projects the power to move people emotionally.”

Categories

From brand design and identity to packaging, spatial communication, websites and sound design – 18 categories are available for participating in the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design.

Spatial Communication
Fair Stands
Retail Design
Websites

Participation, dates and costs

Register in just a few steps via the My Red Dot portal. All important information on the terms and conditions of participation, the registration process, deadlines, and costs can be found here.

Award ceremony

On Friday, 6 November 2026, an unforgettable evening awaits the award winners at the Konzerthaus Berlin. The winners of the Red Dot: Best of the Best will receive their trophies on stage from Professor Dr Peter Zec and a member of the Red Dot jury. Afterwards, the “Designers’ Night” invites guests to an atmospheric after-show party. The evening offers the ideal opportunity to make new contacts, deepen existing acquaintances and celebrate together in a festive atmosphere.

The highlights of the award ceremony on 7 November 2025 can be found here.

Red Dot: Agency of the Year

The honorary title of “Red Dot: Agency of the Year” is awarded annually to an agency that has impressed with outstanding design quality in the Red Dot Award. The agency has demonstrated above-average design achievements either in the context of the current competition or over a period of several years. The title is awarded at the Red Dot Gala in Berlin and is the highlight of the award ceremony. All previous winners can be found in our Hall of Fame.

The “Stylus” challenge trophy

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Accenture Song is Red Dot: Agency of the Year 2025

Red Dot: Junior Award

The Red Dot: Junior Award is part of the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design. This part of the competition is aimed at trainees, students and young professionals who have completed their studies no longer than two years prior to entering the competition. Participants benefit from a reduced entry fee and, in the case of being awarded, a reduced price for the Winner Package.

The highest distinction in the junior competition is the Red Dot: Junior Prize. This award is endowed with 10,000 euros. The winner will be announced at the Red Dot Gala.

In 2025, Ling Tung University was delighted to receive the outstanding award for its work “CICADA’S TUNE.”

Ling Tung University, Red Dot: Junior Prize winner 2025
The “CICADA’S TUNE” project was awarded the Red Dot: Junior Prize
Red Dot Jury

Expertise and experience are combined in the jury members of the Red Dot Award: Brands & Communication Design. Every year, our international experts make a significant contribution to the quality of the Red Dot Label. According to the motto “In search of good design & creativity”, the Red Dot Jury decides on the quality of the entries and thus awards the Red Dot distinctions.

Find an overview of all jury members here.
